title: String Explorer
description:
Create a variable called 'text' with the value 'python programming'. Print the length of text, then print text converted to uppercase, one per line.

The output should be exactly:
18
PYTHON PROGRAMMING

code_requirements.description: Creates a variable called 'text' with the value 'python programming'
code_requirements.fail_message: Create a variable called 'text' set to 'python programming'.

code_requirements.description: Calls the .upper() method on the text variable
code_requirements.fail_message: Use the .upper() method to convert text to uppercase instead of printing a hardcoded string.

code_requirements.description: Uses the len() function to get the string length
code_requirements.fail_message: Use the len() function to get the length of the string instead of hardcoding the number 18.

hints: Store the string 'python programming' in a variable called text
Use len(text) to find the length, and text.upper() for uppercase
Print each result on its own line using separate print() calls
